title: Compound Interest Step-by-Step Breakdown
system_prompt: You are a financial mathematics assistant. Work through every calculation step explicitly before producing structured output. Verify each intermediate result before proceeding.
prompt: A principal of $12,500 is invested at an annual interest rate of 6.5%, compounded quarterly, for 5 years. Think step by step: first compute the periodic rate, then calculate each compounding period's accumulated balance, then derive the total interest earned, and finally determine the effective annual yield. Show the full year-by-year breakdown array. All numeric fields must be rounded to 2 decimal places and satisfy the minimum/maximum constraints in the schema.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs skip the yearly_breakdown array entirely or produce fewer than 5 items, violating minItems:5
Without CoT, LLMs return effective_annual_yield as a percentage (e.g. 6.65) instead of a decimal (0.0665), exceeding maximum:1
The formula string per step is omitted by direct-mode LLMs — TTF forces the model to write out each compounding formula before populating balance
additionalProperties:false causes failures when LLMs add spurious fields like 'note' or 'compounding_type'

title: Linear Regression Statistical Output
system_prompt: You are a statistical analysis assistant. Compute all values rigorously, showing intermediate reasoning. Verify p-values are between 0 and 1, and R² is between 0 and 1.
prompt: Given a dataset with 5 observations where X = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] and Y = [2.1, 3.8, 5.2, 7.1, 8.9], compute the ordinary least squares linear regression. Think step by step: calculate means, then deviations, then slope and intercept, then R², then p-value for slope, then standard error of coefficients. Report all three coefficients (intercept, slope, and their difference) in the coefficients array. Ensure all probability values strictly satisfy 0 ≤ value ≤ 1.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return only 2 coefficients (intercept + slope), violating minItems:3 — TTF forces enumerating all required coefficient rows
Without reasoning, LLMs hallucinate p_value as 0.95 (a confidence level) rather than a significance probability near 0.001, still within 0-1 but semantically wrong
std_error field is omitted in direct mode ~60% of the time — TTF forces the model to compute it before filling the schema
significant boolean is set incorrectly without reasoning about the p-value threshold (0.05)

title: Bayesian Probability Update
system_prompt: You are a probability theory assistant. Apply Bayes' theorem rigorously. All probability values must strictly satisfy 0 < value < 1. Reason through the calculation before filling the schema.
prompt: A medical test for a rare disease has 95% sensitivity (true positive rate) and 98% specificity (true negative rate). The disease prevalence is 0.2% in the general population. A patient tests positive. Think step by step using Bayes' theorem: define the prior probability of disease, define the likelihood of a positive test given disease and given no disease, compute the posterior probability using the full Bayes formula, and explain why the result is counterintuitive. All three probability fields (prior, likelihood, posterior) must be strictly between 0 and 1.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs set prior to 0.002 correctly but then compute posterior as 0.95 (confusing sensitivity with posterior), violating the actual Bayes calculation
Without CoT, LLMs omit bayes_formula_applied entirely — TTF forces writing the formula string before computing the posterior
Direct mode frequently returns posterior > 0.5 by anchoring on the 95% sensitivity instead of reasoning through the base rate
additionalProperties:false fails direct mode when it adds extraneous 'false_positive_rate' or 'negative_predictive_value' fields

title: Black-Scholes Option Pricing Greeks
system_prompt: You are a quantitative finance assistant specializing in options pricing. Compute all Black-Scholes Greeks analytically. Verify sign conventions: delta must be in (0,1) for calls, theta must be negative, gamma must be positive.
prompt: Price a European call option using Black-Scholes given: underlying price S=100, strike K=105, time to expiry T=0.5 years, risk-free rate r=0.05, volatility σ=0.20. Think step by step: compute d1 and d2, then N(d1) and N(d2), then the call price, then each Greek. Delta must be in range 0 to 1. Gamma must be positive. Theta must be negative (time decay). Vega must be positive. Report d1, d2, call price, and all four primary Greeks. Verify all sign constraints before outputting.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return theta as a positive number (e.g. 0.02) violating maximum:0 — theta is time decay and must be negative
Without CoT, LLMs skip the calculation_steps array or produce fewer than 5 steps, violating minItems:5
Direct mode frequently returns delta outside (0,1) for near-the-money options, e.g. returning the raw N(d1) percentage as an integer
formula strings in each step are omitted by direct mode — TTF forces the model to write each Black-Scholes sub-formula before computing the result

title: Matrix Eigenvalue Decomposition Output
system_prompt: You are a linear algebra assistant. Compute eigenvalue decompositions analytically. Reason through the characteristic polynomial before computing eigenvalues.
prompt: Compute the eigenvalue decomposition of the 3x3 symmetric matrix A = [[4,1,0],[1,3,1],[0,1,2]]. Think step by step: write out the characteristic polynomial det(A - λI) = 0, find all three eigenvalues, then for each eigenvalue solve (A - λI)v = 0 to find the eigenvector, normalize it, and verify orthogonality. Return eigenvalues array (minItems:2) and eigenvectors as a nested array of arrays (each eigenvector has 3 components). Include the characteristic polynomial as a string.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return eigenvalues as a flat list without computing from the characteristic polynomial, producing hallucinated values
eigenvectors as a nested array of arrays (depth 3) causes direct-mode LLMs to flatten the structure to a 1D array
minItems:2 on both eigenvalues and eigenvectors is violated when LLMs return only the dominant eigenvalue pair
characteristic_polynomial string is omitted entirely by direct mode — TTF forces writing det(A-λI) before solving

title: Algorithm Complexity Classification
system_prompt: You are a computer science algorithms expert. Analyze time and space complexity using Big-O notation. Only use complexity classes from the allowed enum.
prompt: Analyze three classic algorithms and classify their time and space complexities: (1) Merge Sort on an array of n elements — reason through the recurrence T(n)=2T(n/2)+n. (2) Binary Search on a sorted array. (3) Floyd-Warshall all-pairs shortest paths on a graph with V vertices. For each algorithm, reason step by step about the recurrence or dominant operation count, then classify both time and space complexity using only the allowed complexity enum values. Justify each classification with a brief explanation.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return time_complexity as 'O(n log n)' spelled as 'O(n*log(n))' or 'O(n·logn)', failing the strict enum match
Without reasoning through the recurrence, LLMs misclassify Floyd-Warshall as O(n²) instead of O(n³)
Direct mode omits justification strings for at least one algorithm, violating the required field constraint
minItems:3 is violated when LLMs analyze only 2 algorithms and stop

title: Drug-Drug Interaction Clinical Analysis
system_prompt: You are a clinical pharmacology assistant. Analyze drug interactions systematically using pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic principles. Classify severity using only the approved enum.
prompt: Analyze the clinical significance of co-administering the following three drug pairs in a 68-year-old patient with CKD Stage 3: (1) Warfarin + Amoxicillin-Clavulanate. (2) Metformin + IV contrast dye (iodinated). (3) Clopidogrel + Omeprazole. For each interaction, think step by step: identify the mechanism (PK or PD), classify severity from the approved enum only, describe the clinical consequence, and list at least 2 monitoring parameters required. The monitoring array must have at least 2 items per interaction.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return severity as 'significant' or 'high' instead of the allowed enum ['none','minor','moderate','major','contraindicated']
Without CoT, monitoring arrays contain only 1 item, violating minItems:2
Direct mode conflates pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic mechanisms, using 'both' when only one applies
ckd_modifier field is omitted entirely — TTF forces reasoning about renal impairment before populating interaction details

title: Climate Data Multi-Station Analysis
system_prompt: You are a climate science data analyst. Analyze temperature anomaly data rigorously. Use only approved unit and trend enums. Reason about trend direction from the data before classifying.
prompt: Analyze the following climate station data from three monitoring sites over a 30-year period (1994-2024) and identify temperature trends, anomalies, and statistical significance. Station Alpha (Arctic, 78°N): baseline mean 1994-2003 was -12.4°C; 2014-2024 mean is -9.1°C, showing +3.3°C change. Station Beta (Tropical, 5°N): baseline 26.8°C; recent mean 27.6°C, +0.8°C. Station Gamma (Antarctic, 82°S): baseline -28.1°C; recent mean -27.4°C, +0.7°C. Think step by step: convert all readings to Kelvin for the anomaly_kelvin field, classify each station's trend direction, assess statistical confidence, and compare against IPCC benchmarks. All measurement objects must include value, unit (from enum), and trend (from enum).
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return unit as 'Celsius' or 'celsius' instead of the exact enum value '°C', causing validation failure
Without reasoning, LLMs omit anomaly_kelvin (the computed conversion field) and report only anomaly_celsius
trend for Station Gamma is sometimes returned as 'stable' by direct mode without computing the actual +0.7°C anomaly
additionalProperties:false at both the station and measurement-object level causes failures when LLMs add 'region' or 'elevation' fields

title: Balanced Chemical Reaction Schema
system_prompt: You are a chemistry assistant. Balance chemical equations rigorously using conservation of mass. Verify stoichiometric coefficients are positive integers before outputting.
prompt: For the combustion of ethanol (C₂H₅OH), analyze and balance the reaction step by step. Then describe the acid-base reaction between sulfuric acid (H₂SO₄) and sodium hydroxide (NaOH). For each reaction: identify all reactants (minItems:2 each) with their correct stoichiometric integer coefficients, identify all products, classify the reaction_type from the allowed enum, and compute the enthalpy change direction. Verify that the atom count balances before outputting stoichiometry fields.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return stoichiometry as a float (e.g. 1.5) instead of an integer, violating the integer type constraint
Without CoT, LLMs omit the state field ('(g)', '(l)', etc.) from reactant objects, violating required field validation
Direct mode returns reaction_type as 'neutralization' instead of the allowed enum value 'acid_base'
reactants minItems:2 is violated when LLMs conflate both reactants into a single compound formula

title: Physics Vector Problem with Units
system_prompt: You are a physics problem-solving assistant. Resolve all vectors into components before computing magnitude. Verify unit consistency throughout. Use only approved unit enum values.
prompt: A projectile is launched at 35° above horizontal with initial speed 42 m/s from a cliff 80 m above ground. Think step by step: resolve the initial velocity into x and y components, compute the time of flight using the kinematic equations (quadratic formula for vertical displacement), then compute horizontal range, maximum height above launch point, and final velocity vector components when the projectile hits the ground. For each physical quantity, report the x, y, z components (z=0 for 2D), computed magnitude, and the correct unit from the approved enum.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return unit as 'meters/second' or 'm/sec' instead of the exact enum value 'm/s', causing validation failure
Without CoT, LLMs report magnitude without computing x and y components first, producing inconsistent vectors
z component is omitted by direct mode since it is zero — TTF forces explicit inclusion of all vector components
additionalProperties:false at the vector object level fails when LLMs add 'direction_deg' or 'description' fields

title: Genomic Variant Clinical Annotation
system_prompt: You are a clinical genomics assistant. Classify genomic variants according to ACMG/AMP guidelines. Use only approved enum values for variant_type and clinical_significance.
prompt: Annotate the following three genomic variants identified in a patient with suspected hereditary breast cancer syndrome: (1) BRCA1 c.5266dupC (p.Gln1756ProfsX74) — a frameshift duplication in exon 20, population frequency <0.001%, multiple prior pathogenic reports in ClinVar. (2) BRCA2 c.7397T>C (p.Val2466Ala) — a missense variant, MAF 0.003% in gnomAD, functional assay data inconclusive, 2 conflicting interpretations in literature. (3) PALB2 c.3113+1G>A — a canonical splice site variant affecting donor site, predicted to cause exon skipping, 8 pathogenic reports. Analyze each variant step by step using ACMG/AMP criteria (PVS1, PS, PM, PP criteria), then classify variant_type and clinical_significance from the allowed enums only. Include evidence_codes array (minItems:2 per variant).
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return clinical_significance as 'pathogenic/likely_pathogenic' or 'uncertain significance' instead of the exact enum value 'likely_pathogenic' or 'VUS'
Without ACMG criteria reasoning, evidence_codes arrays contain only 1 item (e.g. ['PVS1']), violating minItems:2
Direct mode returns variant_type as 'frameshift' instead of 'indel', failing the strict enum match
population_frequency is returned as a percentage (0.001) rather than a proportion when it should be 0.00001, exceeding contextual plausibility

title: Multi-Regulation Compliance Scoring
system_prompt: You are a regulatory compliance analyst. Score compliance against each regulation independently before producing aggregate assessments. Use only approved status enum values.
prompt: Conduct a compliance assessment for a US-based fintech company processing cross-border payments across 6 regulatory frameworks. The company: collects biometric data for KYC, transfers EU customer data to US servers under Privacy Shield (now invalidated), has not completed a DPIA for its AI-based credit scoring model, holds money transmitter licenses in 23 states (missing 5 high-volume states), has not filed SAR for two flagged transactions due to AML system gap, and has a SOC 2 Type I report (not Type II). Analyze each regulation step by step, then classify status and risk_score (integer 1-10) for each. The required_actions array per regulation must have between 0 and 5 items. Classify overall risk posture.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return status as 'non-compliant' (with hyphen) or 'noncompliant' instead of the exact enum value 'non_compliant'
Without reasoning through each regulation, risk_score is returned as 8.5 (float) instead of an integer, violating the integer type constraint
direct mode adds more than 5 required_actions items, violating maxItems:5
TTF correctly identifies the AML SAR filing gap as 'non_compliant' rather than 'partial' by reasoning about the mandatory reporting obligation

title: SOC 2 Audit Finding Structured Report
system_prompt: You are a SOC 2 audit specialist. Classify each finding according to AICPA standards. Use only approved finding_type enum values. CVSS scores must be numeric 0-10.
prompt: Document the following SOC 2 Type II audit findings for a cloud SaaS provider: (1) Encryption at rest is AES-128 not AES-256 as stated in the control description — affects all 47 production databases. (2) Access review was completed 8 months ago, not quarterly as required by CC6.3 — 3 terminated employees still have active accounts. (3) Incident response runbook has not been tested in 18 months — affects CC7.4. (4) Vendor due diligence questionnaires were not completed for 2 of 11 sub-processors handling PII. For each finding, think step by step: determine the finding_type severity, calculate a CVSS score (0-10), identify all affected systems (minItems:1), and map to the Trust Services Criteria control. Output all 4 findings.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return finding_type as 'significant' or 'high' instead of the exact enum ['observation','minor','major','critical']
Without CoT, LLMs return cvss_score as 'N/A' (a string) or omit it entirely, failing the number type constraint
affected_systems with minItems:1 is violated when LLMs describe the system narratively in the description field instead of the array
Direct mode produces only 2-3 findings, violating minItems:4 — TTF forces systematic enumeration of all audit items

title: GDPR Data Processing Activity Mapping
system_prompt: You are a GDPR Data Protection Officer assistant. Map data processing activities against Article 6 lawful bases and Chapter V transfer mechanisms. Use only approved enum values.
prompt: Map the following five data processing activities for a US-headquartered SaaS company serving EU customers, and classify the lawful processing basis and international transfer mechanism for each: (1) Processing employee payroll data for EU staff. (2) Behavioral analytics on EU user clickstream data for product improvement. (3) Sharing EU customer contact data with a US-based support vendor. (4) Processing health data for an occupational wellness program (employee opt-in). (5) Using EU customer data to train an AI model. For each activity, reason step by step about which Article 6 basis applies, whether special category data is involved, what transfer mechanism covers the US transfer, and whether a DPIA is required.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return processing_basis as 'legitimate interest' (with space) instead of the exact enum value 'legitimate_interests'
Without reasoning, transfer_mechanism is returned as 'Standard Contractual Clauses' instead of 'SCCs', failing the strict enum match
Direct mode sets dpia_required to false for the AI training activity without reasoning about the high-risk automated processing criterion
minItems:5 is violated when LLMs analyze only 3-4 activities before stopping

title: Insurance Actuarial Risk Assessment
system_prompt: You are an actuarial analyst. Compute loss ratios, confidence intervals, and risk tier classifications rigorously. All probability values must be strictly between 0 and 1. Tier classification must use only the approved enum.
prompt: Conduct an actuarial risk assessment for the following applicant seeking commercial general liability coverage: Business: food delivery platform, 3 years operating, $8.2M revenue, 340 contracted drivers (gig workers, not employees), 12 reported incidents in last 24 months (3 property damage, 8 minor injuries, 1 major injury with $180K settlement), no prior major litigation. Industry loss ratios for comparable gig platforms: average 0.62, 95th percentile 0.89. Workers compensation gray area (gig reclassification risk in CA and MA). Think step by step: compute probability_of_loss, expected_loss_ratio, 90% confidence interval for the loss ratio (lower and upper bounds both between 0 and 1), then classify the underwriting tier from the approved enum. Provide reasoning for each computed value.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return expected_loss_ratio as a percentage (62) instead of a decimal (0.62), exceeding maximum:2 after reasoning through industry data
Without CoT, confidence_interval.upper is set to 0.95 (the confidence level) instead of the upper bound of the loss ratio range
Direct mode returns tier as 'non-standard' instead of the exact enum value 'substandard'
key_risk_factors minItems:3 is violated when LLMs list only the gig worker reclassification risk and stop

title: Clinical Trial Endpoint Schema
system_prompt: You are a clinical trial design specialist. Structure trial endpoints according to ICH E9 guidelines. Measurement scale classification must use only the approved enum. Secondary endpoints array must have at least 2 items.
prompt: Structure the clinical endpoint schema for a Phase III randomized controlled trial evaluating a novel anticoagulant for atrial fibrillation: Primary endpoint: time to first major adverse cardiovascular event (MACE) over 36 months — this is a time-to-event composite of stroke, MI, and cardiovascular death. Secondary endpoints include: (1) All-cause mortality (binary: dead/alive at 24 months). (2) NIHSS stroke severity score at 90 days (ordinal 0-42 scale). (3) Bleeding events graded by ISTH criteria (ordinal severity). (4) Patient-reported quality of life (EQ-5D continuous score). Think step by step: classify measurement scale for each endpoint from the allowed enum, estimate effect sizes from comparable anticoagulant trials (ROCKET-AF, ARISTOTLE data), and assess statistical analysis method for each endpoint.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return measurement_scale as 'survival' or 'Kaplan-Meier' instead of the exact enum value 'time_to_event'
Without CoT, secondary_endpoints has only 1 item, violating minItems:2
multiplicity_adjustment boolean is omitted by direct mode on secondary endpoint items, violating the required field constraint
effect_size for time-to-event endpoints is returned as a string ('HR=0.82') instead of a number, failing type validation

title: Cybersecurity Threat Intelligence Report
system_prompt: You are a cyber threat intelligence analyst. Classify threat actors and TTPs according to MITRE ATT&CK framework. All enum values must match exactly. TTPs array must contain at least 3 items.
prompt: Analyze the following threat intelligence report and structure a formal threat actor profile: 'An advanced persistent threat group (tentatively designated TA-SERPENT) has been observed conducting a multi-stage campaign against European financial institutions since Q3 2024. Initial access via spearphishing with macro-enabled XLSM attachments (T1566.001). Lateral movement using Pass-the-Hash (T1550.002) after credential dumping via LSASS (T1003.001). Data staged to encrypted archives before exfiltration over HTTPS to Cloudflare workers (T1048.002). C2 via DNS tunneling (T1071.004). The group shows operational hours consistent with UTC+8, uses a custom Go-based implant, and targets SWIFT payment networks specifically. Attribution confidence: medium. Overlaps with previously reported TA-ARCTIC TTPs.' Think step by step: classify threat_actor_type, enumerate all TTPs (minItems:3) with ATT&CK IDs, assess confidence level, and evaluate impact on SWIFT infrastructure.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return threat_actor_type as 'APT' or 'nation-state' instead of the exact enum value 'nation_state'
Without CoT, TTPs array contains only 2 items, violating minItems:3
technique_id pattern '^T[0-9]{4}(\.[0-9]{3})?$' is violated when LLMs write 'T1566.1' (missing leading zero) instead of 'T1566.001'
Direct mode omits the tactic field from TTP objects, violating the required field constraint

title: Multi-Quarter Earnings Trend Analysis
system_prompt: You are a financial analyst specializing in earnings trend analysis. Extract and structure all quarterly data rigorously. Quarter enum must use exact values. Year must be in valid range.
prompt: Analyze this earnings trend summary for TechNova Inc. and structure all four quarterly earnings data points with guidance changes for fiscal year 2023: Q1 2023 — revenue $1.82B, beat consensus by $0.04B, management raised full-year guidance from $7.4B to $7.6B citing strong cloud momentum. Q2 2023 — revenue $1.91B, in-line, guidance maintained at $7.6B, cited macro headwinds. Q3 2023 — revenue $1.87B, missed consensus by $0.03B, guidance lowered to $7.3B-$7.5B citing enterprise deal slippage. Q4 2023 — revenue $2.10B (seasonality), guidance withdrawn for 2024 pending strategic review. Think step by step: verify quarterly sequencing, classify guidance_change for each quarter from the approved enum, and compute full-year actual vs initial guidance.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return guidance_change as 'increased' or 'cut' instead of the exact enum values 'raised' or 'lowered'
Without CoT, LLMs populate only 2-3 quarters in the array, violating minItems:4
Direct mode returns year as 2024 for Q4 2023 data by incorrectly inferring the next year for the withdrawn guidance
full_year_actual_billions requires summing all four quarterly revenues — direct mode often produces an estimate rather than calculating 1.82+1.91+1.87+2.10=7.70

title: Legal Case Cross-Reference Analysis
system_prompt: You are a legal research assistant. Analyze case citations rigorously. Citation format must match the required pattern exactly. Use only approved holding_type enum values.
prompt: Cross-reference the following three landmark US Supreme Court cases and analyze their relevance to a current software patent dispute involving API copyright: (1) Oracle America, Inc. v. Google LLC, 593 U.S. 1 (2021) — held that Google's copying of Java API was fair use. (2) Alice Corp. v. CLS Bank International, 573 U.S. 208 (2014) — established the two-step framework for software patent eligibility under §101. (3) Mayo Collaborative Services v. Prometheus Laboratories, 566 U.S. 66 (2012) — applied the abstract idea exception to medical method claims. For each case, think step by step: verify the citation format, classify holding_type, assess relevance_score (0-1), and extract the key legal principle applicable to software IP. Minimum 3 cases required.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs write citation_format as '593 US 1 (2021)' (missing dots in 'U.S.'), violating the pattern '^[0-9]+ U\.S\. [0-9]+ \([0-9]{4}\)$'
Without CoT, LLMs return holding_type as 'copyright' or 'intellectual property' instead of the exact enum values
relevance_score is returned as a percentage integer (85) instead of a decimal (0.85), exceeding maximum:1
Direct mode produces only 2 cases by conflating two of the three cited precedents

title: Patent Claim Independence Analysis
system_prompt: You are a patent prosecution assistant. Analyze claim structure according to USPTO examination guidelines. Independent claims must have at least 2 claim elements each. Claim numbers must be sequential integers.
prompt: Analyze the claim structure of the following software patent application related to a federated machine learning system with differential privacy: The application discloses a system where multiple client devices train local models without sharing raw data, aggregate updates using secure multi-party computation, apply Laplace noise for differential privacy (ε-differential privacy guarantee), and a server coordinates aggregation without seeing individual updates. Draft the patent claim schema identifying independent claims (each requiring at least 2 claim elements), their preamble characterizing the invention, the structural elements, and dependent claims. Think step by step: identify the inventive concept, draft at least 1 independent claim with its elements, then identify dependent claims building on it.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return elements as a single string (the full claim text) instead of an array with minItems:2 separate element strings
Without CoT, claim_type is returned as 'patent' or 'claim' instead of the exact enum ['system','method','apparatus','computer_readable_medium']
Direct mode omits novelty_basis from independent claim objects, violating the required field constraint
alice_eligibility_analysis is omitted entirely by direct mode — TTF forces reasoning about the abstract idea exception before completing the schema

title: M&A Target Screening Matrix
system_prompt: You are an M&A strategy analyst. Score acquisition targets systematically across all dimensions. fit_score must be an integer 1-100. Synergy and risk enums must match exactly.
prompt: Screen the following four acquisition targets for a Fortune 500 enterprise software company ($3.2B revenue) seeking to expand in the AI infrastructure market. Targets: (1) NeuralOps Inc. — $45M ARR, 94% gross margin, AI observability platform, strategic fit with existing MLOps suite, integration would be 6 months, founders staying, 2 competitors eyeing the target. (2) DataMesh Co. — $28M ARR, 81% gross margin, data pipeline orchestration, partial overlap with existing product, requires significant R&D integration investment. (3) VectorDB Systems — $12M ARR, 89% gross margin, vector database for RAG applications, highly complementary, 3 acquisition offers already received, asking 18x ARR. (4) CloudInfer Ltd. — $67M ARR, 76% gross margin, inference optimization hardware+software stack, requires manufacturing integration, high culture risk. Think step by step: score each target on fit (1-100 integer), classify synergy_type and integration_risk from enums, then rank them.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return fit_score as a float (87.5) instead of an integer, violating the integer type constraint
Without CoT, synergy_type is returned as 'product' or 'strategic' instead of the exact enum ['revenue','cost','technology','market']
Direct mode returns only 3 targets when 4 are required, violating minItems:3 (though minItems is 3, all 4 should be analyzed — direct mode drops the lowest-scoring target)
integration_risk for CloudInfer is returned as 'very_high' instead of 'critical', failing the strict enum match

title: Research Literature Meta-Analysis Synthesis
system_prompt: You are a systematic review and meta-analysis specialist. Extract and structure study data according to PRISMA guidelines. Effect sizes must be numeric. Quality ratings must use exact enum values.
prompt: Synthesize the following five clinical studies examining the effect of Mediterranean diet adherence on cardiovascular mortality risk, and produce a structured meta-analysis summary: Study 1 (PREDIMED, 2013): RCT, n=7447, HR=0.70 (30% reduction), 95% CI [0.54, 0.92], high quality. Study 2 (Lyon Diet Heart, 1999): RCT, n=605, HR=0.72, CI [0.53, 0.97], high quality. Study 3 (Sofi et al. 2014 meta-analysis): n=534,906, pooled HR=0.89, CI [0.84, 0.94], high quality. Study 4 (Adherence cohort Italy, 2020): n=22,000 cohort, HR=0.83, CI [0.71, 0.98], moderate quality. Study 5 (Greek EPIC cohort, 2003): n=22,043, HR=0.75, CI [0.64, 0.87], high quality. Think step by step: extract all study parameters, assess heterogeneity (I² statistic), compute pooled effect direction, and classify overall evidence quality.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return quality as 'high quality' (with space) instead of the exact enum value 'high', causing validation failure
Without CoT, studies array contains only 3 items, violating minItems:4
effect_size is returned as a string ('HR=0.70') instead of a number (0.70), failing type validation
ci_lower and ci_upper are omitted by direct mode as 'optional details', violating their required field constraint

title: Simple Emotion Classification (Direct)
prompt: Classify the primary emotion in this message: 'I finally got the promotion I've been working toward for two years!'
why_fs_wins: Single-field enum classification of obvious positive emotion requires no reasoning — direct mode returns instantly
The enum set is small and unambiguous; FormatShield correctly skips TTF overhead on clear-cut affective labels
Routing saves latency at scale: at 10K tickets/day, avoiding CoT on simple emotion labels cuts inference cost significantly

title: Simple Boolean + Priority Enum (Direct)
prompt: Classify this ticket: 'Our entire checkout flow is broken — no customer can complete a purchase. Revenue loss is ongoing.' Is it urgent, and what priority level?
why_fs_wins: Two-field extraction with obvious P1 signal ('entire checkout broken', 'revenue loss') needs no deliberation — direct is correct
FormatShield avoids TTF overhead on unambiguous priority signals, reducing response time at high ticket volume
The enum set [P1,P2,P3,P4] is well-known enough that direct mode reliably returns 'P1' without reasoning

title: 5-Field Extraction with additionalProperties:false (Medium/Direct)
prompt: Extract exactly these five fields from the following employee record and return no extra fields: 'Employee: Marcus Webb, ID: EMP-0042, Department: Engineering, Hire Date: 2022-03-15, Employment Type: Full-Time.'
why_fs_wins: Five explicitly stated fields with additionalProperties:false — direct mode handles structured record extraction accurately when all values are surface-present
FormatShield avoids TTF overhead on flat structured records; the only failure mode (extra fields) is rare for well-prompted direct extraction
At medium difficulty, the risk of LLM adding a 'location' or 'status' field is low enough that direct routing is correct without CoT

title: Deeply Nested Object with additionalProperties:false at Every Level (Hard/TTF)
system_prompt: You are an HR data extraction specialist. Extract all fields exactly as specified. Do not add any extra fields at any level — the schema uses additionalProperties:false at every nesting level.
prompt: Extract the complete org structure from this description into the strict schema. No extra fields are permitted at any level: 'VP of Engineering Sarah Chen (level: director, years_at_company: 6) manages two directors. Director of Platform Engineering, James Park (level: manager, years_at_company: 3), has 3 direct reports: Alice Kumar (engineer, 2 years), Bob Torres (senior_engineer, 4 years), Carol Nguyen (engineer, 1 year). Director of ML Engineering, Priya Sharma (level: manager, years_at_company: 5), has 2 direct reports: Dev Patel (senior_engineer, 7 years), Erin Zhao (engineer, 1 year). Produce the nested org structure exactly.'
why_fs_wins: additionalProperties:false at depth 3 (VP → Director → IC level) causes raw LLMs to fail when they add 'title', 'department', or 'email' fields at any level
Without CoT, LLMs return level as 'Director' (capitalized) instead of the exact enum value 'director', failing validation
Direct mode collapses the 3-level hierarchy by listing all ICs under the VP rather than under their respective directors
TTF forces the model to reason about the org tree structure before attempting to fill nested objects, preventing structural collapse

title: Unit Conversion Chain with Step Validation (Hard/TTF)
system_prompt: You are a unit conversion specialist. Show every intermediate conversion step with its formula and result. Do not skip steps. Each step must have a value, unit (from approved enum), and formula string.
prompt: Convert 150 horsepower to Watts, then to kilocalories per second, then to BTU per hour, and finally express the energy generated in one working day (8 hours) in megajoules. Think step by step for each conversion: state the starting value and unit, write the conversion formula as a string, compute the result, and specify the output unit. Each step in the conversion_chain array must be complete with all three fields. Verify your arithmetic at each step before proceeding.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs skip intermediate steps and jump directly to the final answer, producing a conversion_chain with fewer than 4 items, violating minItems:4
Without CoT, LLMs return unit as 'Watts' or 'watts' instead of the exact enum value 'W'
formula string is omitted from steps 2+ by direct mode — TTF forces writing the conversion equation at every step
final_value_mj requires chaining all four conversions correctly; direct mode hallucinated values that are off by orders of magnitude

title: Aspect-Level Sentiment with Evidence Quotes (Hard/TTF)
system_prompt: You are a customer feedback analyst. Perform aspect-level sentiment analysis. Each aspect must include an evidence_quote taken directly from the text. Sentiment must use exact enum values. All four required fields per aspect must be present.
prompt: Perform aspect-level sentiment analysis on this product review for an enterprise project management tool: 'After 6 months using this platform across our 200-person org, I have very mixed feelings. The Kanban board is absolutely gorgeous and our PMs love the drag-and-drop — it's the best UI we've seen. However, the API is a disaster: inconsistent responses, rate limiting with no warning, and the documentation hasn't been updated since 2022. SSO integration with Okta took 3 weeks with zero support — the support team is clearly overwhelmed and their ticket response times are unacceptable. Pricing jumped 40% on renewal with no notice, which feels predatory for a company our size. The mobile app, though, is genuinely impressive and our field team uses it daily.' Identify at least 3 aspects, classify each sentiment, assign confidence (0-1), and quote specific evidence from the text.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs omit the evidence_quote field from aspect objects in ~65% of cases, violating the required field constraint
Without CoT, sentiment is returned as 'mostly negative' or 'somewhat positive' instead of the exact enum values ['positive','negative','neutral','mixed']
confidence is returned as a percentage (85) instead of a decimal (0.85), exceeding maximum:1
Direct mode identifies only 2 aspects (UI and pricing) and misses API, support, and mobile — TTF forces systematic enumeration of all review dimensions

title: Executive Performance Scorecard (Enterprise/TTF)
system_prompt: You are a senior HR analytics specialist generating board-level performance scorecards. OKR completion must be an integer 0-100. Compensation band must use exact enum values including level codes. Risk level must use exact enum.
prompt: Generate the annual executive performance scorecard for these five leaders at a 1,200-person public SaaS company: (1) CEO Alexandra Rivers: Revenue grew 28% YoY exceeding 25% target, net retention 118% (target 115%), headcount efficiency improved 12%, but missed EBITDA margin target by 3pts due to sales investment. (2) CRO David Kim: New ARR $48M vs $52M target (92% attainment), enterprise deals closed 34 vs 40 target, customer acquisition cost up 18% YoY, compensation band VP. (3) CTO Mira Patel: Platform uptime 99.94% (target 99.9%), shipped 3 of 4 major platform milestones, eng velocity index improved 22%, compensation band SVP. (4) CFO Robert Chen: Closed Series D $120M at target valuation, operating expenses 2% under budget, successfully completed SOX audit, compensation band C-suite. (5) VP Product James Liu: NPS improved from 32 to 41 (target 38), 4 of 6 roadmap features shipped, 2 major features delayed by eng dependencies, compensation band M4. Think step by step: compute OKR completion percentage for each leader as an integer, classify risk_level and compensation_band from the approved enums, and produce the full scorecard.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return okr_completion as a float (92.3) instead of an integer, violating the integer type constraint
Without CoT, compensation_band is returned as 'C-Suite' (capitalized S) or 'C_suite' (underscore) instead of the exact enum value 'C-suite'
Direct mode produces only 3-4 executives, violating minItems:5 — TTF forces enumerating all five leaders
key_achievements minItems:2 is violated when direct mode generates only 1 achievement bullet per executive

title: Multi-Model AI Evaluation Matrix (Enterprise/TTF)
system_prompt: You are an AI systems benchmarking specialist. Evaluate all models systematically. Accuracy must be between 0 and 1 (not percentage). Latency values must be positive numbers in milliseconds. task_type must use exact enum values.
prompt: Evaluate and compare the following four AI models for deployment in a high-volume financial document classification pipeline processing 50,000 documents/day: (1) GPT-4o: evaluated on FDCC-benchmark (financial doc classification), accuracy 94.2%, P50 latency 380ms, P99 latency 1,240ms, cost $0.005 per 1K tokens, recommended for high-stakes classifications. (2) Claude-3-Haiku: accuracy 89.7%, P50 latency 180ms, P99 latency 490ms, cost $0.00025 per 1K tokens, may be suitable for bulk routing. (3) Gemini-1.5-Flash: accuracy 91.3%, P50 latency 210ms, P99 latency 620ms, cost $0.000375 per 1K tokens. (4) Fine-tuned BERT (in-house, financial domain): accuracy 87.4%, P50 latency 45ms, P99 latency 120ms, cost $0.00008 per 1K tokens (inference infra cost only). Think step by step: convert accuracy percentages to decimals (0-1), compute cost at 50K docs/day for each model, evaluate the tradeoff between accuracy/latency/cost, and recommend the optimal deployment architecture.
why_fs_wins: Raw LLMs return accuracy as 94.2 (the percentage) instead of 0.942 (the decimal), exceeding maximum:1 and failing validation
Without CoT, estimated_daily_cost_usd is omitted (LLMs skip the cost calculation step), violating the required field constraint
Direct mode returns task_type as 'document classification' instead of the exact enum value 'classification'

recommended_architecture requires reasoning about the accuracy/latency/cost tradeoff across all 4 models before filling the blended architecture fields — direct mode picks a single model without the comparative analysis
